title: 3 Things AMC Stock Must Do to Bounce Back in 2026
text: The piece examines three critical requirements for AMC Entertainment to recover in 2026 after experiencing severe stock declines over four consecutive years. The shares have fallen 41% in 2025 alone. First, achieving profitability is essential—AMC must match competitors like Cinemark and Imax, which have maintained profitability since 2023. The article notes that AMC hasn't turned an annual profit since 2018, despite occasional profitable quarters. Second, controlling share dilution is critical. The company has dramatically expanded its share count from 11.8 million to 440.6 million since 2019—a 37-fold jump. For comparison, Cinemark's count increased 24% and Imax's actually declined 10% over the same period. Third, pursuing real innovation is necessary. Management must move beyond gimmicks like credit cards and cryptocurrency acceptance toward genuine business transformation, as revenue has declined and free cash flow remains negative. The article concludes that without meaningful reinvention, AMC's path forward remains uncertain.
